summary: Policy-backed control for DNS client NetBIOS name resolution behavior. The current app uses the strict disable value, and the local Microsoft DnsClient.admx file explicitly documents the same registry key, value name, and enum values.

setting.name: DNS Client NetBIOS Name Resolution
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can use NetBIOS-style name resolution through the DNS client policy layer. This policy lets you fully disable it, allow it, or choose more limited modes.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they want a more modern and predictable name-resolution posture. They keep broader modes when legacy discovery behavior still matters.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it fully off can reduce legacy name-resolution behavior, but some older network discovery flows may stop working. Unlike the separate NetBIOS over TCP/IP interface setting, this policy is a DNS client behavior control and supports more than one mode.
setting.professional_notes: DnsClient.admx defines DNS_Netbios as an enum-based policy writing EnableNetbios. The current app writes 0, which is the strict disable value. Other supported values are 1, 2, and 3.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force a DNS client NetBIOS policy mode.

allowed_values.label: Disable
allowed_values.meaning: Disable NetBIOS name resolution for the DNS client policy.

allowed_values.label: Disable on public networks
allowed_values.meaning: Disable NetBIOS name resolution on public networks only.

allowed_values.label: Learning mode
allowed_values.meaning: Use the learning mode defined by the DNS client policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes EnableNetbios = 0, which matches the strict disable mode documented in the local ADMX enum.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows Vista and newer systems with the DNS client policy available

states.rationale: The policy is enum-backed and not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This is a policy baseline. It is different from the separate per-interface NetBIOS over TCP/IP setting.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Environments where NetBIOS client policy behavior should stay untouched
avoid_for: Users who want explicit DNS client hardening
recommended_profiles.good_when: You do not want to force a NetBIOS client policy mode.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You intentionally want to disable NetBIOS name resolution.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps its normal behavior for this policy area.
No explicit hardening mode is enforced.

recommended_profiles.label: Strict disable
intended_for: Users who want the most direct disable mode
avoid_for: Users who still need NetBIOS client fallback
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to remove this legacy name-resolution path as much as possible.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: Some network discovery path still relies on it.
tradeoffs: The app currently only exposes this strict mode, not the more nuanced enum choices.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable on public networks
intended_for: Users who want a more nuanced policy than full disable
avoid_for: People who want a single simple hard-disable

states.rationale: Use the public-networks-only disable mode defined by the ADMX enum.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to document the richer policy options even if the app does not expose them yet.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You expect the current app to support this mode directly today.
tradeoffs: The current app does not expose this value as a preset yet.
recommended_profiles.notes: Documented for research completeness. Not currently exposed as a first-class preset in the app.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_DnsClient Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for ADMX-backed DNS client policies.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DnsClient.admx NetBIOS enum mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file defines DNS_Netbios as an enum that writes EnableNetbios with values 0, 1, 2, and 3.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes EnableNetbios = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft DnsClient.admx file explicitly defines DNS_Netbios under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\DNSClient with enum valueName EnableNetbios, and the app writes one of the documented enum values.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="DNS_Netbios" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\DNSClient"> <enum id="DNS_Netbios_Box" valueName="EnableNetbios" required="true"> <item displayName="$(string.DNS_Netbios_Disable)"><value><decimal value="0" /></value></item>
validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same registry key and value name that the app writes, and it exposes the enum values 0, 1, 2, and 3.
